segments.text: Every AI tutorial out there tells you
the same thing. Learn to ask better
questions. But what if asking questions
is the problem? I took my actual
business expense data and gave it to
Claude with zero questions. And it
surfaced patterns I'd completely missed
for months. Insights I never would have
thought to look for because I didn't
even know they existed. I'm going to
show you the exact method and why the
best insights usually come from
questions you don't even know to ask. So
let's get into it. So the way that most
people utilize AI is they start with a
very specific question. So they can give
it a data set such as expenses, revenue,
even a bunch of transcripts from
meetings. They give it that data and
then they ask targeted questions. And by
doing that, you're holding back the AI's
true power. Now, here's an analogy that
I wanted to walk you through. So we have
all these dots on the right hand side.
Imagine each one of these dots being a
skill or a power that the AI has. When
you ask a very targeted question, the AI
is only going to focus all of its power
on that very specific skill set to
answer the question you've asked. So,
you're constraining the AI, which in
turn reduces the value you get back from
the AI, no matter if it's doing analysis
on data sets for revenue and expenses or
like I said, transcripts for meetings or
even assessing employee performance. The
way that we can fix this is by instead
of asking a targeted question, we give
the AI our intent and then get out of
its way. By doing that in this analogy,
the AI is then going to have a lens.
Instead of focusing on a very specific
dot, it's going to have this entire lens
here of all the different skills that it
can leverage to then provide back to you
more value that you wouldn't get in the
previous case because the AI is a
world-class data analyst and probably
knows a lot more about your industry
than others would. So, it can combine
those two skills to then connect dots
you wouldn't necessarily know to connect
for the specific intent you provided to
the AI. So, that's our analogy. Let's
get more concrete with this. There's a
three-phase approach that I take with
any of my clients and myself when
exploring data. The key word here that I
just stated is explore. So you can see
the very first step of the three-phase
process is the explore process. So we're
going to give the AI access to our data.
Once we've get it access to the data,
it's going to then get the intent. So
we're going to provide our intent as
well. Once we provided those two things,
it'll explore for a bit. After the
explore process, it's going to make a
series of discoveries. Some of which may
be interesting, some of which may not.
those areas that are interesting to you,
the things that surprise you and its
discoveries, that's where you then start
to constrain the AI's abilities with
specific questions. And it's important
to mention that the issue isn't the
specific questions that are being asked,
but when you ask those questions. So,
you can see in this process, we
constrain the AI with those specific
questions at the end after it's done the
discovery. Okay? So, if you're enjoying
this, you're going to enjoy two other
things. First off, below is a 30-day AI
insight series, completely free. You'll
get 30 insights in your inbox how you
can apply AI to your business and your
work. The second thing is if you'd like
to work with me, blower a series of
offerings to see if there's a good fit
between the two of us, such as private
one-on-one coaching for AI or a private
community for AI business leaders and
owners. With that being said, let's get
back into the video. And that's the
beautiful part about this process. So,
what I'm going to do here is I'm going
to show you two approaches. We have two
methods of how we can use this process.
One is the basic method that all of you
can do probably in about 2 minutes. And
the other one's a bit more involved, but
also provides a lot more value. So
method one is using the basic chat app.
So chatbt, Gemini, cloud, all these
tools that you use day in and day out.
The second method is going to be using a
tool like cloud co-work or codeex where
the AI actually comes to the data
instead of you bringing the data to the
AI. And I'll talk through the
limitations of both. So let's get into
method one. So method one, all we have
to do is take this prompt, paste it into
chatbt Gemini or claude, give it the
files that you wanted to assess, and
then walk away. I'll briefly walk you
through the prompt and then call out
some things to consider when you're
working through this specific method. So
in this prompt, we're stating that you
are a business analyst that specializes
in Xindustry. So this is the industry
that you're going to fill in based off
of the specific analysis it's doing for
you. We tell it that we've hired it to
audit my data. And then I'm going to
state some other things. So I'm going to
say I'm the blank. So give it your role.
And then this is my intent. So in my
case, I'm the CEO. And my intent here is

the money effectively to grow and we're
not wasting too much of it. After we've

should be doing, which is exploring the

then for each finding, I'm asking the AI
to tell me what it found, why it
matters, and anything that I should do
about it. And after that, I'm asking the
AI to go beyond the obvious. So this is

multiple files, and then you just get

first before you ever constrain the AI.
Now, choose some of the caveats that I

note that with method one, you're

can only take in so many files and so
many files that are so big. So, you're
limited both both by the quantity and
the size. And here's a good rule of

type is a CSV, it's likely going to be
lighter. So, that means the AI can spend
more time thinking instead of just
reading the file. After that, we have
markdown files and txt files, which are
a little bit heavier than CSVs, but not
too heavy. After that, we have Excel
files. So these are going to be Excel,
Google Sheets, etc. Especially if there
are multiple columns, multiple rows, and
multiple tabs. Those can get extremely
overwhelming for AI. So it's important
to note if there is something that
you're interested in inside of an Excel
sheet, just download that one tab as a
CSV and pass it off to the AI to do the
data analysis. And then finally, we have
PDFs, which can take up a lot of space
in the AI's head and its memory
specifically for usually two reasons.
One is if the file is massive andor if
the file is very complex. So, if it's a
PDF that has a bunch of screenshots,
annotations, complex diagrams, things
like that, the AI is going to struggle
more with it if it's too big. So, it's
important to keep this in mind, not to

andor too complex and or too many files
because usually you're capped around 10
files per conversation with these tools.
But note that these limitations are just
for method one, not method two. So, it's
important to note this is just a method
one thing. And then after you've gotten

the data, and you let it do the analysis

somewhere else. So it can keep working
on stuff until it's done. So I'm going
to walk you through my example of how

is create a single folder somewhere on

After you've named it, then you're going

After that, the AI will actually go off

what are these instructions that I'm

And these instructions are actually
something that you've probably used in

And then I want you to create a findings

and then basically keep going through
